name: Pipe Flow Calculator
description: Industrial-grade pipe flow analysis tool based on Darcy-Weisbach, Bernoulli, and empirical friction factor correlations. Compliant with ISO 5167, ASME MFC, and Six Sigma measurement system analysis principles.

inputs.label: Pipe Inner Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Nominal pipe diameter per ISO 6708. Affects cross-sectional area and velocity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Nominal pipe diameter per ISO 6708. Affects cross-sectional area and velocity.

inputs.label: Pipe Length
inputs.businessContext: Total straight pipe length. Used for friction loss calculation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total straight pipe length. Used for friction loss calculation.

inputs.label: Volumetric Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Design flow rate. Must be within pump capacity and pipe rating.

businessContext_i18n.en: Design flow rate. Must be within pump capacity and pipe rating.

inputs.label: Fluid Density
inputs.businessContext: Density at operating temperature. Water = 1000, oil ≈ 850.

businessContext_i18n.en: Density at operating temperature. Water = 1000, oil ≈ 850.

inputs.label: Dynamic Viscosity
inputs.businessContext: Dynamic viscosity at operating temperature. Water = 0.001, heavy oil up to 1.

businessContext_i18n.en: Dynamic viscosity at operating temperature. Water = 0.001, heavy oil up to 1.

inputs.label: Pipe Roughness
inputs.businessContext: Absolute roughness height. Steel = 0.000045, concrete = 0.001. Per ISO 9906.

businessContext_i18n.en: Absolute roughness height. Steel = 0.000045, concrete = 0.001. Per ISO 9906.

inputs.label: Elevation Change
inputs.businessContext: Positive if outlet is higher than inlet. Affects gravitational head.

businessContext_i18n.en: Positive if outlet is higher than inlet. Affects gravitational head.

inputs.label: Minor Loss Coefficient (K)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of K factors for fittings, valves, bends. Per Crane Technical Paper No. 410.

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of K factors for fittings, valves, bends. Per Crane Technical Paper No. 410.

inputs.label: Flow Regime Assumption
inputs.businessContext: Select 'auto' for Reynolds-based determination. Override for sensitivity analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select 'auto' for Reynolds-based determination. Override for sensitivity analysis.

inputs.label: Include Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.businessContext: When true, applies GUM (Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement) propagation to final result.

businessContext_i18n.en: When true, applies GUM (Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement) propagation to final result.

rules.message: Flow rate, diameter, and viscosity must be positive to compute Reynolds number.
Relative roughness exceeds 0.05; Colebrook equation may be inaccurate. Consider using Moody chart directly.
Velocity exceeds 10 m/s; risk of erosion and water hammer. Consider larger diameter.

thresholds.message: Pressure drop exceeds 5 bar. Verify pump head capacity and pipe rating.
Fully turbulent flow with high roughness; friction factor may be sensitive to small changes in roughness.
Flow is laminar but flow rate is relatively high. Verify viscosity measurement.
